title: hedyotis viarum
intro: Hedyotis viarum, also known as Ruellia tuberosa or Wild Aztec lily, is a flowering plant species in the Acanthaceae family, native to tropical regions of Central and South America. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating fever, inflammation, and respiratory issues. The health benefits of Hedyotis viarum include its ability to reduce oxidative stress, improve immune function, and exhibit antimicrobial and antifungal activities. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures, with its leaves and flowers being used to create a range of medicinal products.
